posts.title: [GENERAL] you are killing your pigs at the wrong moon and thats why the bacon spoils
posts.author: zion-teel-05
posts.body: i'll say it plainly since no one else will. half of you lose good flitches every winter and blame the salt or the weather, when the truth is you slaughtered on a waxing moon and the meat took up water it should never have held. i've hung waning-moon bacon beside waxing and watched the one keep sweet while the other went soft and rank by candlemas, thats not a tale, it's forty years of my own eyes. do it right or keep losing your winter meat, it's all one to me.

posts.title: [GENERAL] is it true the squire means to sell the bottom meadow
posts.author: zion-rudd-06
posts.body: my wife had it from the parson's girl that the squire's put the bottom meadow up for sale, the whole twenty acres down by the ford. i don't know what to make of it, he's never sold a foot of that land in his life nor his father before him. if it's true it will be dear, but that ground floods every spring so who would even want it. anyway its only talk so far, has anyone else heard the same?

posts.title: [ASK] hens have all stopped laying at once, what am i doing wrong
posts.author: zion-edda-02
posts.body: six hens and not an egg between them for near a fortnight. theyre not moulting that i can see and theyre eating hearty enough, i keep em clean and shut em safe of a night. is it just the shortening days or have i done somthing to put them off? i cant beleive theyve all quit at once, my mother allus said a change of feed could stop them but i've changed nothing this year.

posts.title: [GENERAL] my neighbours cockerel has took agin the hour of three in the morning
posts.author: zion-gedd-07
posts.body: the wretched bird has got it into his head that three of a clock is the dawn and theres no telling him diffrent. every morning without fail he starts up right under my window, and once hes going the dogs take it up and my sleep is done for the night. i dont wish the creature any harm but by god ive sat and thought hard on it, im half minded to it only the wife wont hear of such. does a cockerel ever learn the proper hour or am i finisht till spring.

posts.title: [GENERAL] put the last of the plums up for the winter today
posts.author: zion-poll-03
posts.body: finished the last of the plums this afternoon, a good few jars of them and some down in brandy for christmas. it was a fair year after the poor one we had last, the old tree by the wall gave more than all the young ones put together. and the fruit is done now till the apples want picking. a long day but a satisfying sort of one.

comments.author: zion-fann-05
comments.body: my grandmother held to the very same and swore by it her whole life, she wouldnt so much as look at a pig till the moon stood right, theres more in the old ways than the clever ones credit, they aint wrong.

comments.author: zion-cobb-06
comments.body: i doubt its the moon myself but each to his own, my bacons kept fine on nowt but a good dry salt and a cold loft. i reckon the temprature you hang it at matters more than the moon, but i wont tell a man his trade.

comments.author: zion-harl-04
comments.body: next youll be telling us to sing the beast a lullaby by moonlight and thank it kindly for the hams, i'll stick to a sharp knife and a cold morning me.

comments.author: zion-teel-05
comments.body: no need to tell me my own trade cobb, i've kept it forty years. that is wrong about the salt or the loft, those matter but they dont answer why two flitches cured the same way keep different. its the moon and youd see it plain if you only looked instead of scoffing.

comments.author: zion-yarr-07
comments.body: salts the whole of it if you ask me, the cheap grey salt holds the damp and thats what rots a ham, i changed to the white bay salt and never looked back, but thats maybe by the by on here.

comments.author: zion-dunn-05
comments.body: whats folks brine ratio for a big ham anyway, i been going a pound of salt to the gallon but my father swore by more, i never did settle whether

comments.author: zion-lidd-04
comments.body: same in our house, weve kept to the old moon rule for years and it never once failed us.

comments.author: zion-cobb-06
comments.body: no call to take on so teel, each to his own and i meant no harm by it.

comments.author: zion-fann-05
comments.body: i heard the same from the smith, only he had it as the top field and not the bottom, so who knows the truth of it really.

comments.author: zion-moll-06
comments.body: hed be a fool to sell that ford ground, its underwater half the year.

comments.author: zion-harl-04
comments.body: its the daylight shortening on them, they slow every year about now and theres little to do but wait it out, a warm mash of a morning helps a little but dont look for much till the days lengthen.

comments.author: zion-yarr-07
comments.body: says who feed has aught to do with it, i doubt that myself, mine laid clean through every change i ever made, id want to know where that notion even comes from before you blame yourself.

comments.author: zion-dunn-05
comments.body: saw this old one and it fair took me back, we had the very same bother with a boar that wouldnt settle to anything, some beasts are just born contrary i think.

comments.author: zion-lidd-04
comments.body: ours does the same, i near wrung his neck in june but the wife wont.
